# Implementation Plan: Demo Audio Coaching

**Branch**: `main` | **Date**: 2026-06-24 | **Spec**: [`spec.md`](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-demo-audio-coaching/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/spec.plan` workflow and stops at planning/design artifacts. Implementation tasks belong in `tasks.md`.

## Summary

Harden the existing audio-agent slice so the demo can reliably capture browser microphone audio, transcribe it into timestamped transcript segments, detect obvious filler words and pacing issues, and preserve report access when microphone capture or STT fails. The implementation should reuse the current browser PCM WebSocket pipeline, FastAPI `AudioAgent`, Orchestrator-owned database writes, `audio_warnings`/`transcript_entries` persistence, `DEMO_MODE=replay` fixtures, and final report scoring instead of adding new infrastructure.

The main technical work is reliability: make replay/fallback idempotent, avoid duplicate prepared events, keep session isolation intact, make filler/pacing detection deterministic and testable, keep live warnings throttled, and validate the report path under live, replay, and no-audio scenarios.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11+ backend; TypeScript 5.6 + React 18/TanStack Start frontend; Node 20+; pnpm 9; uv-managed backend environment  
**Primary Dependencies**: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy async, PostgreSQL/asyncpg, Pydantic v2, httpx, OpenAI-compatible LiteLLM client, ElevenLabs Scribe v1 direct fallback, TanStack Start/Router, native browser MediaDevices/WebSocket/Web Audio APIs  
**Storage**: PostgreSQL 15 via existing `sessions`, `transcript_entries`, `audio_warnings`, and `reports` tables; prepared replay JSON fixtures under `packages/backend/fixtures/`  
**Testing**: Backend `uv run pytest`; backend lint `uv run ruff check .`; frontend type/lint `pnpm --filter @octoprep/web-dashboard lint`; repo shortcuts `make test` and `make lint` when all package scripts are healthy  
**Target Platform**: Local demo stack with FastAPI on port 8000, TanStack Start dashboard on port 3000, and modern desktop Chromium-family browser for microphone capture  
**Project Type**: Web application with frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL persistence, browser-to-backend WebSockets, and report UI  
**Performance Goals**: 2-second audio chunks; transcript-backed voice insight visible within 30 seconds after session end for a 30-60 second script; complete report reachable within 60 seconds even when live STT fails; live warnings debounced enough to avoid distracting toast spam  
**Constraints**: One-day hackathon/demo scope; no new external services or queues; Orchestrator remains sole database writer; capability-token auth required for audio/feedback sockets; final report remains primary feedback surface; color is not the sole meaning indicator; fallback must be explicit and non-duplicative  
**Scale/Scope**: Single-presenter demo sessions, short 20-60 second rehearsals, English speech, prepared fallback events acceptable as demo insurance

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Assessment |
|-----------|--------|------------|
| I. Demo-First Vertical Slices | PASS | The feature protects the core flow: start session, capture audio, end session, render `/session/:id/report`. P1 report feedback is planned before optional live warning polish. |
| II. Contracted Agent Boundaries | PASS | AudioAgent emits typed `TranscriptPayload` and `AudioWarningPayload`; Orchestrator owns PostgreSQL writes. Any payload additions must update backend Pydantic and shared TypeScript contracts together. |
| III. Session Isolation and Explicit Sharing | PASS | Audio and feedback WebSockets continue to require `session_id` plus `access_token`; report access remains token/share-token gated. Planning calls out rejecting writes to ended sessions. |
| IV. Resilience Before Polish | PASS | Provider errors, empty STT, microphone failure, and replay mode are first-class validation paths. `DEMO_MODE=replay` remains available as demo insurance. |
| V. Native Stack, Minimal Abstractions | PASS | Reuses native browser audio/WebSocket APIs, FastAPI async handlers, existing SQLAlchemy models, and JSON replay fixtures. No new package, queue, or storage layer is planned. |

**Gate Result**: PASS. No constitution violations require justification.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/002-demo-audio-coaching/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── audio-coaching-contract.md
└── tasks.md                 # Created later by /spec.tasks, not by this plan
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
packages/
├── backend/
│   ├── agents/
│   │   ├── audio_agent.py          # STT, filler detection, WPM warnings, replay dispatch
│   │   ├── replay_fixtures.py      # Prepared demo audio events
│   │   ├── report_agent.py         # Voice scoring and insight aggregation
│   │   └── schemas.py              # Pydantic agent/report contracts
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── models.py               # transcript_entries, audio_warnings, reports
│   │   └── repository.py           # Orchestrator-facing persistence methods
│   ├── fixtures/
│   │   └── audio_events.json       # Demo fallback transcript/warning fixtures
│   ├── routers/
│   │   ├── audio_ws.py             # Browser PCM chunk input
│   │   ├── feedback_ws.py          # Live feedback output
│   │   └── sessions.py             # Session end and report retrieval
│   └── tests/
│       ├── test_wav_header.py
│       ├── test_ws_auth.py
│       └── test_report_dedup.py
├── shared-types/
│   └── src/index.ts                # Frontend/shared WebSocket and report payload types
└── web-dashboard/
    └── app/
        ├── lib/capture.ts          # Browser mic capture and PCM chunking
        ├── lib/ws.ts               # Audio/feedback WebSocket clients
        └── routes/
            ├── session.$id.tsx
            └── session.$id_.report.tsx
```

**Structure Decision**: Use the existing monorepo structure. Backend work stays under `packages/backend/agents`, `routers`, `db`, and `tests`; shared payload contract changes go through `packages/shared-types`; frontend behavior stays in session capture/report routes and existing capture/WebSocket helpers.

## Phase 0 Research Summary

Research output is captured in [`research.md`](./research.md). Key decisions:

- Keep fixed browser-to-backend audio format: 16 kHz mono 16-bit little-endian PCM chunks over `/audio-stream`.
- Wrap PCM as WAV server-side before Scribe/STT calls.
- Use LiteLLM STT first, direct ElevenLabs fallback when `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` exists, and `PROVIDER_MODE=direct` as an emergency flip.
- Emit replay audio fixtures once per session and treat fixture source data as immutable.
- Deduplicate at write-time and report-time for prepared transcripts and audio warnings.
- Keep no-audio outcomes explicit instead of blocking session completion or inventing hidden data outside `DEMO_MODE=replay`.

## Phase 1 Design Summary

Design output is captured in:

- [`data-model.md`](./data-model.md)
- [`contracts/audio-coaching-contract.md`](./contracts/audio-coaching-contract.md)
- [`quickstart.md`](./quickstart.md)

The design preserves existing tables and contracts where possible. The only acceptable contract changes are explicitly documented in the contract artifact before implementation: adding an audio-unavailable warning type is optional and should be skipped unless required for a user-facing persisted backend failure event.

## Post-Design Constitution Check

| Principle | Status | Assessment |
|-----------|--------|------------|
| I. Demo-First Vertical Slices | PASS | Artifacts validate P1 final report feedback first, P2 fallback second, and P3 live warnings last. |
| II. Contracted Agent Boundaries | PASS | Contracts document AudioAgent-to-Orchestrator payloads and frontend feedback events; no direct agent database writes are introduced. |
| III. Session Isolation and Explicit Sharing | PASS | Contracts require `session_id` and token for all audio/feedback sockets and forbid cross-session replay leakage. |
| IV. Resilience Before Polish | PASS | Quickstart includes live STT, replay, microphone denial, and duplicate-replay validation scenarios. |
| V. Native Stack, Minimal Abstractions | PASS | Design relies on existing native/browser/backend mechanisms and rejects new queues, packages, or services for demo scope. |

**Gate Result**: PASS. No unresolved clarifications and no constitution violations.

## Triage Framework: [SYNC] vs [ASYNC] Classification

**Execution Strategy**: Use a hybrid execution model. Humans should review session lifecycle, auth, and demo behavior; agents can implement deterministic tests, fixture hygiene, and small UI copy changes once contracts are fixed.

### Preliminary Task Classification

| Task Category | Estimated [SYNC] Tasks | Estimated [ASYNC] Tasks | Rationale |
|---------------|----------------------|----------------------|-----------|
| Business Logic | 3 | 4 | Audio replay idempotency, filler policy, and pacing thresholds affect demo credibility and need review; deterministic helpers/tests are safe to delegate. |
| Data Operations | 2 | 2 | Write-time dedup keys and ended-session write prevention require careful DB/session review; repository tests can be delegated. |
| UI Components | 1 | 2 | User-facing failure copy needs human judgment; live-warning display and manual responsive checks are low-risk. |
| Integrations | 3 | 2 | STT fallback order, provider timeout behavior, and WebSocket auth/session state are high-risk; mocked provider tests can be delegated. |
| Infrastructure | 1 | 1 | No new infrastructure; update validation scripts/docs and keep existing commands. |

### Triage Decision Criteria Applied

**High-Risk [SYNC] Classifications:**

- Decide whether to add a persisted `AUDIO_UNAVAILABLE`/provider-failure warning type or keep failures as local UI/report outcomes.
- Review replay activation semantics so prepared demo evidence never appears accidentally outside `DEMO_MODE=replay`.
- Review auth/session-state enforcement for `/audio-stream` after session end.
- Review final voice score semantics for insufficient audio because the current contract requires numeric `voice_score`.

**Agent-Delegated [ASYNC] Classifications:**

- Add unit tests for `_wrap_pcm_as_wav`, filler matching, WPM thresholds, debounce behavior, and replay fixture immutability.
- Add mocked STT fallback tests for gateway-first and direct-first modes.
- Add report dedup tests for repeated fallback transcript/warning rows.
- Update frontend copy for microphone denial/disconnection after copy is approved.

### Triage Audit Trail

| Task | Classification | Primary Criteria | Risk Level | Rationale |
|------|----------------|------------------|------------|-----------|
| Replay audio fixtures emit once per session | [SYNC] | Demo correctness, state lifecycle | High | Current per-chunk replay can visibly duplicate transcript/warning evidence. |
| Fixture loader avoids mutating cached rows | [ASYNC] | Deterministic bug fix | Low | Small isolated helper change with straightforward tests. |
| Filler detection policy for `um`/`uh` and ambiguous `like` | [SYNC] | Product judgment | Medium | False positives hurt demo trust; policy should match script. |
| WPM sustained-window threshold tests | [ASYNC] | Algorithm validation | Medium | Deterministic tests can pin behavior once thresholds are chosen. |
| `/audio-stream` rejects ended or unauthorized sessions | [SYNC] | Security/session isolation | High | Capability-token boundary is constitutional. |
| Live warning throttling | [ASYNC] | UX polish with tests | Medium | Useful but should not block final report behavior. |
| No-audio report outcome | [SYNC] | Contract semantics | High | Existing `voice_score` is numeric, so behavior must be intentional and documented. |

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations. No added package, service, queue, storage layer, or framework abstraction is justified for this feature.
